text.title: langchain-agents-multi-agent Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for multi-agent LangGraph systems.

rule.statement: Supervisor agents MUST route via `model.with_structured_output(Route)` (Pydantic Literal on `agent`); free-text routing strings are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Structured routing eliminates parse failures and makes routing audit-trail-able.

rule.statement: Debate patterns MUST have a hard round cap (default 5) AND a consensus threshold; either alone is insufficient.
rule.rationale: Without both, debate loops indefinitely or produces low-quality forced consensus.

rule.statement: Specialist node logic MUST be unit-testable with mocked LLM calls; the node MUST be a pure function of `state -> partial state`.
rule.rationale: Logic mixed with LLM side-effects cannot be unit-tested; integration tests alone are too slow for CI.

rule.statement: Hierarchical team subgraphs MUST share a parent-state-compatible TypedDict; subgraph schemas MUST be a strict superset of the parent's reads.
rule.rationale: Mismatched fields cause silent state loss or KeyError when the parent reads subgraph output.

rule.statement: Supervisor prompts that include user input MUST escape or template-isolate it; raw user input cannot manipulate routing.
rule.rationale: Routing prompts that interpolate user input directly let a malicious task description re-route to an unintended specialist.

rule.statement: Every routing decision MUST be logged to LangSmith with metadata tags (`session_id`, `target_agent`, `reason`); cross-session cost attribution requires this.
rule.rationale: Routing patterns are invisible without tracing; debugging mis-routes is impossible without per-call evidence.
